info.name: Pawfront — Pet Parent API
info.description:
Postman collection for the Pawfront pet-parent host (Pawfront.PetParentApi). All endpoints are under /api/v1 and require a Firebase ID token from the pet-parent Firebase project (`littersoftpetparent`) — passed as `Authorization: Bearer {{idToken}}`.

**Setup**
1. Set `baseUrl` (default `https://localhost:7216`).
2. Get a pet-parent Firebase ID token (see CLAUDE.md `Get a fresh Firebase ID token`) and set `idToken`.
3. Run the folders top-to-bottom — they are ordered chronologically (onboarding → profile → pets → discovery → bookings → events). Most requests carry a **test script that auto-captures ids** (`petParentId`, `petId`, `parentMobileOtpId`, `providerId`, `serviceId`, `bookingId`, `eventId`, `eventBookingId`, `petParentPhotoId`) into collection variables, so you rarely need to paste a GUID by hand.

**Notes**
- Response envelope is `{ success: bool, data: T | null, error: { code, message } | null }`.
- The `/pet-parents/{petParentId}/*` and `/pets/{petId}/*` routes are ownership-filtered: the caller's PetParentId is derived from the JWT, so the route id must be your own.
- Provider **discovery / search / booking** only return data if a provider has been registered via the provider host (`Pawfront.Api`). Run that collection first to seed a provider, or paste a known `providerId` / `serviceId`.
- Image-upload requests use multipart with field name `file` — attach a local file before sending.
- `serviceId` is the booked service. Searches return `serviceId` per hit; their test scripts capture the first one.

variable.description: Captured by the provider searches / discovery. Or paste one from the provider host.
Captured by the provider searches (first hit's serviceId).
PetGroomer menu code (one of the 18 canonical codes).
Captured by GET /events (first hit) and POST /pet-parents/{petParentId}/events.
Set to any URL returned by an upload endpoint to test POST /blob-images.

request.description: Upserts Parent.ParentAuthIdentities + (optional) device token from the JWT. All body fields optional. Call this FIRST after sign-in.

body.raw:
{
  "firstName": "Jane",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "gender": "Female",
  "mobileCountryCode": "+41",
  "mobileNumber": "791234567",
  "dateOfBirth": "1990-05-12",
  "addressLine": "Bahnhofstrasse 1",
  "latitude": 47.3769,
  "longitude": 8.5417,
  "zipCode": "8001",
  "city": "Zurich",
  "description": "Loving pet parent of one happy dog."
}

request.description: Creates Parent.PetParents and links to the auth identity (resolved from JWT sub/user_id — NOT from the body). Captures `petParentId`. 404 ParentAuthIdentityNotFound if `firebase-auth` was never called; 409 MobileNumberAlreadyExists; 400 UnsupportedGender.

Resolves the caller's Firebase uid → { parentAuthIdentityId, petParentId?, firebaseUserId, email, isEmailVerified, displayName, signUpStatus, hasProfile, mobileVerifiedAtUtc? }. Used by mobile after reinstall to recover the PetParentId. Re-captures `petParentId` if a profile exists.
Full profile read-back (name, gender, email + isEmailVerified, mobile, DOB, address, lat/lng, zip, city, description, profilePhotoUrl, mobileVerifiedAtUtc, timestamps). 404 PetParentNotFound.

Edits the basic-profile subset. NOT editable here: mobile number (re-verify via OTP), latitude/longitude, profile photo (own endpoint). Returns the full read-back shape. 404 PetParentNotFound; 400 UnsupportedGender / InvalidRequest.
multipart/form-data — attach a `file` (≤3 MB, JPEG/PNG/WebP). 400 InvalidFile/ImageTooLarge/UnsupportedImageFormat; 404 PetParentNotFound.
General pet-parent photo gallery. One row per upload — call N times for N photos. Captures `petParentPhotoId`. ≤3 MB, JPEG/PNG/WebP. 404 PetParentNotFound.
Lists the parent's gallery photos, oldest-first ([] when none).
Removes one gallery photo (scoped by PetParentId + PhotoId) + best-effort blob delete. 404 PetParentPhotoNotFound.
multipart/form-data. file ≤3 MB, JPEG/PNG/WebP; identityType ∈ {Passport, DriverLicense, NationalId, ResidencePermit}. One identity per parent — re-uploading replaces.

Reads the parent's single identity row with full details + the document blob URL: { parentIdentityId, petParentId, identityType, identityPhotoUrl, createdAtUtc, updatedAtUtc }. Fetch the document bytes by POSTing identityPhotoUrl to /blob-images. 404 ParentIdentityNotFound when none on file.
Removes the parent's single identity row + best-effort blob delete. 404 ParentIdentityNotFound.
Returns { basicInfo, profilePhoto, pets, petMedicalInfo, identity, verification, isFullyOnboarded }.
Generates a 6-digit OTP (10-min expiry, NoOp sender today). Captures `parentMobileOtpId`. Returns { parentMobileOtpId, mobileCountryCode, mobileNumber, dateSentUtc, expiresAtUtc }.
Always 200; client branches on { isValidated, validationStatus: Validated|Invalid|Expired|Pending }. (The dev OTP is not 123456 — read it from the API logs / sender, or replace here.)
Captures `petId`. petType ∈ {Dog, Cat, Hamster, GuineaPig}; gender ∈ {Male, Female}; weight DECIMAL(5,2) > 0; microchipId globally UNIQUE (409 MicrochipIdAlreadyExists — change it if it collides).
Returns all pets with embedded photo gallery (oldest-first). Empty array if none — list semantics, no 404.

Single pet profile — full basic-info + medical-info snapshot + embedded photo gallery (oldest-first), same shape as the list endpoint. Ownership-filtered: 404 PetNotFound (unknown pet), 403 Forbidden (someone else's pet).
Edits the basic-info subset only — medical-info untouched. Same shape as AddPet.

vaccinationStatus ∈ {Vaccinated, NotVaccinated}; sterilizationStatus ∈ {Sterilized, Intact}; temperament ∈ {Anxious, Friendly, Aggressive} but OPTIONAL (omit/empty → null); medicalHistory optional. Only vaccination + sterilization gate onboarding completeness.

multipart/form-data — attach a `file` (≤3 MB, JPEG/PNG/WebP). Sets the pet's SINGLE primary/profile photo (distinct from the gallery below) — stored on Parent.Pets.ProfilePhotoUrl and surfaced as `profilePhotoUrl` on every pet read. Returns { petId, profilePhotoUrl, updatedAtUtc }. Ownership-filtered. 404 PetNotFound; 400 InvalidFile / ImageTooLarge / UnsupportedImageFormat.
multipart/form-data — attach a `file` (≤3 MB, JPEG/PNG/WebP). One row per upload; call N times for N photos. Captures `petPhotoId`.

Permanently removes the pet. Photo rows cascade (blobs left for a future sweep); bookings that referenced the pet are detached (their petId set null, snapshots preserved). Ownership-filtered. Returns { petId, petParentId, deletedAtUtc }. 404 PetNotFound for unknown pet; 403 Forbidden for someone else's pet.

Removes one photo from a pet's gallery (scoped by petId + photoId) + best-effort blob delete. Ownership-filtered. Returns { petPhotoId, petId, photoUrl, deletedAtUtc }. 404 PetPhotoNotFound; 403 Forbidden / 404 PetNotFound on the pet.

item.name: 5. Provider Discovery & Search
item.description: Parent-facing browse + per-service booking search. These return data only if a provider has been registered via the provider host. `petId` (optional) is ownership-enforced and its PetType becomes the animal filter. Each search test script captures the first hit's `providerId` + `serviceId` for the booking + slots folders below. Each per-service search hit also carries `imageUrl` — the service image the provider uploaded for that offering (null when unset).

query.description: Optional. Must be your pet; its PetType becomes the animal filter.
PetSitter | PetGroomer | PetTrainer | Vet (PetAdoptionAndSale is not a valid filter value)
date + startTime + endTime travel as a trio (full free-slot check).

request.description: Slim summary cards. ALL filters optional + combinable. Captures the first hit's `providerId`. NOTE: the old `?serviceCategory=` / `?animals=` params were REMOVED — use `?providerType=` and `?petId=`.

query.description: date+startTime+endTime trio; FULL window must be bookable.

request.description: PetSitter/DayCare search. Charges = PricePerHour. Captures first hit's `providerId` + `serviceId`.

query.description: startDate+pickupDate pair; pickupDate is checkout (not a stayed night); span ≤ 30 nights.

request.description: PetSitter/NightStay search. Every night from startDate..pickupDate-1 must have free capacity. Charges = PricePerHour.

query.description: One of the 18 canonical codes; when set, charges = that item's price.

request.description: PetGroomer/GroomingSession search. With serviceItemCode → only providers offering that ACTIVE item; charges = item price (PerService). Without → any groomer with ≥1 active item; charges null.
Vet/VetAppointment search. date → any free appointment slot that day. Charges = PricePerAppointment (PerAppointment).

query.description: Optional. Must be your pet; its PetType becomes the animal filter.
Optional. date → any free slot of the session's duration that day.

request.description: PetTrainer/TrainingSession search. A training session is a single fixed-duration booking (like a vet appointment): date → any free slot of the session's duration that day. Charges = PricePerSession (PerSession). Each hit carries imageUrl (the offering's service image).

Parent-facing provider profile: registration, category-specific offering, workingHours (7 days), timeOff, minimumHoursBeforeCancellation (null|24|48|72|96), acceptedPaymentMethods (["Cash"|"Digital"], [] when unset), and reviews (always [] for now — review feature not built yet). 404 ProviderNotRegistered.
Free-slot query for PetSitter / PetTrainer / Vet (use durationHours). Closures, capacity, and overlapping confirmed bookings are subtracted per-service.

query.description: One of the 18 canonical PetGroomer codes

request.description: PetGroomer uses serviceItemCode — duration resolved server-side from the menu item. Errors: 400 InvalidServiceId / ServiceItemCodeRequired / ServiceItemNotOffered / InvalidBookingDuration / OfferingNotConfigured; 409 ServiceItemInactive; 404 ServiceNotRegistered.

item.name: 6. Service Bookings (parent-initiated)
item.description: Parent 'book now' against a discovered service. Needs `petId` (your pet) + `serviceId` (from a search). bookingDate / startTime / endTime must fall inside the provider's working hours for the booked service — adjust to a free slot returned by the slots endpoint.

request.description: Booker = route petParentId (from JWT, not the body). Provider resolved from serviceId. petId must be yours. For PetGroomer set `serviceItemCode` (the duration is derived from it). Captures `bookingId`. Errors: 404 PetNotFound / ServiceNotRegistered; 403 Forbidden; 400 InvalidServiceId / InvalidPetId / InvalidBookingTime; 409 ServiceClosed / CapacityExceeded / ProviderInactive.

The parent's own service bookings ("my bookings"), most-recent first (BookingDate/StartTime DESC), cancelled included. Ownership-filtered — petParentId comes from the JWT, so you only ever see your own. Returns [] when none (no 404).
Parent sets APPROVAL_NEEDED | COMPLETED | PARENT_CANCELLED on their own booking; audited. 403 Forbidden; 400 BookingStatusNotAllowed; 409 BookingStatusTerminal | BookingStatusUnchanged.
Full status audit trail, oldest-first. 404 if not your booking.

Single booking read. Returns { booking, startOtp, pendingModification }. When the booking is in a startable (confirmed-equivalent) state, startOtp carries the plaintext code + expiry for the parent to read out to the provider (who enters it on the provider host's /start). When a provider has staged a modification, pendingModification carries the proposed date/time so the parent can accept/decline. 404 if not your booking.
Parent cancels their booking → PARENT_CANCELLED.

Parent proposes a new date/time (editing is limited to the schedule). Stages the proposal → MODIFICATION_REQUEST_BY_PARENT; the provider sees it and accepts/declines. 409 ModificationAlreadyPending if one is already open.
Parent accepts the provider's staged modification → PARENT_ACCEPTED_MODIFICATION; the proposed date/time is applied and the staging row removed. 409 CapacityExceeded / NoPendingModification.
Parent declines the provider's staged modification → PARENT_DECLINED_MODIFICATION; booking keeps its original details, staging row removed.
Read-only: the provider-uploaded job-completion photos, oldest-first (parents view the proof; the provider uploads them).

item.name: 6b. Night-Stay Bookings (multi-night boarding)
item.description: Multi-night boarding (PetSitter NightStay service) is a separate entity from single-day bookings — a stay is a check-in/check-out date RANGE, not a single-day time window. The stay spans [checkInDate, checkOutDate): checkOutDate is the pickup day and is NOT a stayed night. Capacity is enforced per night. Parent-host only (the parent is who books). Provider/serviceId must come from a NightStay search (GET /providers/search/night-stay). The single-day POST /bookings rejects a NightStay serviceId with 400 UseNightStayEndpoint.

request.description: Books a multi-night stay. serviceId must be an active NightStay service; petId must be one of your pets; provider is resolved server-side. checkOutDate must be after checkInDate; max 30 nights; checkOutDate is the pickup day (not a stayed night). DropOff/PickUp times are snapshotted from the offering. Captures nightStayBookingId. Errors: 404 PetNotFound / 403 Forbidden (pet), 400 InvalidServiceId / NotNightStayService / OfferingNotConfigured / InvalidPetId / InvalidNightStayDates, 404 ProviderNotFound / PetParentNotFound, 409 ProviderInactive / CapacityExceeded / ServiceClosed.
Your own night-stay bookings, most-recent first (CheckInDate DESC), cancelled included. Ownership-filtered. [] when none.

Single night-stay booking — returns the detail wrapper { booking, startOtp (when startable), pendingModification (staged date-range proposal, when one awaits a response) }. 404 NightStayBookingNotFound if unknown or not yours.

Parent moves the booking to a new status — one of APPROVAL_NEEDED | COMPLETED | PARENT_CANCELLED (cancel is done here, mirroring single-day; PARENT_CANCELLED frees the per-night capacity). Actor=Parent. 404 NightStayBookingNotFound, 403 Forbidden, 400 BookingStatusNotAllowed, 409 BookingStatusTerminal | BookingStatusUnchanged.
Full status audit trail, oldest-first. 404 if not your booking.
Parent cancels the stay → PARENT_CANCELLED (frees per-night capacity).
Parent proposes a new check-in/check-out range (checkOutDate = pickup day, NOT a stayed night) → MODIFICATION_REQUEST_BY_PARENT. 409 ModificationAlreadyPending if one is already open.

Parent accepts the provider's staged date-range proposal → PARENT_ACCEPTED_MODIFICATION; the range is applied (per-night capacity re-checked) and the staging row removed. 409 CapacityExceeded / NoPendingModification.
Parent declines the provider's staged proposal → PARENT_DECLINED_MODIFICATION; the stay keeps its original dates, staging row removed.
Read-only: the provider-uploaded job-completion photos for the stay, oldest-first.

query.description: AdoptionAndRescue | PetTraining | Charity | Volunteering | HealthAndWellness | SocialAndCultural | OutdoorActivities | ParentEducation
Optional case-insensitive 'contains' search on the event title (LIKE %term%).

request.description: Catalog listing. All query params optional; amenities repeats. `title` is an optional case-insensitive 'contains' search on the event title. Captures the first event's `eventId`. Each event returns top-level `isPaid` / `price` (all event types), `pawPrints`, an `organizer { type, id, name, imageUrl }` block, physical details (incl. location), `eventLink` (online joining URL; null for physical), and a `bookings { maxBookings, totalBookings }` block (maxBookings = physical capacity, null for online; totalBookings = non-cancelled tickets booked).

Top-N trending events, most engaging first. Trending score = ViewCount + ShareCount + non-cancelled (Confirmed) ticket bookings. Same provider-agnostic shape + Cosmos hydration + isBookable (matched by JWT email) as GET /events; no filters. Mirror of the provider host.

Single event detail (SQL + Cosmos for physical). Returns top-level `isPaid` / `price` for every event type (online included), `physical.maximumCapacity` + `physical.location` for physical events, `eventLink` (online joining URL; null for physical), `pawPrints { viewCount, shareCount, inquiryCount }`, an `organizer { type, id, name, imageUrl }` block, a `bookings { maxBookings, totalBookings }` block, `paymentOptions ["Cash"|"Digital"]`, and `attendees [{ attendeeName, ticketNumber }]` (names only — booker contact / payment stay on the organiser dashboard).
Returns updated { viewCount, shareCount, inquiryCount }.

item.name: 8. Parent-organised Events
item.description: A pet parent can create their own events (ProviderId NULL, PetParentId set). Ownership-filtered on petParentId.

request.description: multipart/form-data → { url }. No 1 MB cap (banners can be larger). Captures the URL into `blobUrl` so you can paste it as bannerImageUrl below.

body.raw:
{
  "eventCategory": "OutdoorActivities",
  "isChildFriendly": true,
  "title": "Community Dog Walk 2026",
  "description": "A friendly group walk around the lake for dogs and their humans.",
  "bannerImageUrl": null,
  "amenities": ["FreeParking", "DrinkingWater"],
  "eventType": "Physical",
  "startDate": "2026-08-10",
  "endDate": "2026-08-10",
  "startTime": "09:00:00",
  "endTime": "12:00:00",
  "isPaid": true,
  "price": 50,
  "cancellationPolicy": "FullRefundUpTo2Hours",
  "eventLink": null,
  "physical": {
    "maximumCapacity": 100,
    "location": {
      "houseNumber": "12",
      "street": "Seefeldstrasse",
      "city": "Zurich",
      "zip": "8008",
      "country": "Switzerland",
      "latitude": 47.3667,
      "longitude": 8.5500
    }
  }
}

request.description: Creates a parent-organised event. `isPaid` / `price` are TOP-LEVEL (apply to all event types). `price` is required when `isPaid` is true. For Physical events, `physical.maximumCapacity` AND `physical.location` (houseNumber/street/city/zip/country required, lat/long optional) are required. Captures `eventId`. 404 PetParentNotFound; 400 InvalidRequest.

body.raw:
{
  "eventCategory": "ParentEducation",
  "isChildFriendly": true,
  "title": "Webinar: First-time Puppy Care",
  "description": "Live online Q&A for new pet parents.",
  "bannerImageUrl": null,
  "amenities": ["None"],
  "eventType": "Online",
  "startDate": "2026-08-20",
  "endDate": "2026-08-20",
  "startTime": "19:00:00",
  "endTime": "20:30:00",
  "isPaid": false,
  "price": null,
  "cancellationPolicy": "NoRefund",
  "eventLink": "https://meet.example.com/puppy-care-webinar",
  "physical": null
}

request.description: Online event — no `physical` block (not bookable). `isPaid` / `price` still apply at the top level (here it's free). Online paid events are allowed too (e.g. a paid webinar). `eventLink` is the joining URL — optional, returned only for online events (dropped/null for physical).

body.raw:
{
  "eventCategory": "OutdoorActivities",
  "isChildFriendly": true,
  "title": "Community Dog Walk 2026 (Updated)",
  "description": "Updated route — now starting from the north gate.",
  "bannerImageUrl": null,
  "amenities": ["FreeParking", "DrinkingWater", "Restrooms"],
  "eventType": "Physical",
  "startDate": "2026-08-11",
  "endDate": "2026-08-11",
  "startTime": "08:30:00",
  "endTime": "11:30:00",
  "isPaid": true,
  "price": 60,
  "cancellationPolicy": "FullRefundUpTo4Hours",
  "eventLink": null,
  "physical": {
    "maximumCapacity": 120,
    "location": {
      "houseNumber": "12",
      "street": "Seefeldstrasse",
      "city": "Zurich",
      "zip": "8008",
      "country": "Switzerland",
      "latitude": 47.3667,
      "longitude": 8.5500
    }
  }
}

request.description: Full-replace edit of a parent-organised event — every field editable (same body shape as create). Ownership-filtered; 404 EventNotFound when not found or not owned by this parent; 400 InvalidRequest on validation. Reconciles the Cosmos physical doc (upsert when physical; deletes the old doc when the event becomes Online or its category changes). Editing a paid event to free drops its payout methods. Returns the full detail (incl. bookings, paymentOptions, attendees).

Partial edit — send ONLY the fields you want to change; everything else stays as-is. Each field is optional; an OMITTED field is left unchanged, while an explicit null CLEARS it (e.g. `"bannerImageUrl": null`). The merged event is then validated like a full edit, so cross-field rules still apply (flipping `eventType` to Physical needs a `physical` block; flipping `isPaid` to true needs a `price`). Ownership-filtered. 404 EventNotFound; 400 InvalidRequest. Returns the full detail (incl. bookings, paymentOptions, attendees).

Lists the events this parent has organised (mirror of the provider host's GET /providers/{providerId}/events). Ownership-filtered. Hydrates physical details (incl. location) per event. Each event includes pawPrints { viewCount, shareCount, inquiryCount }, an organizer { type, id, name, imageUrl } block, and a bookings { maxBookings, totalBookings } block (maxBookings = physical capacity, null for online; totalBookings = non-cancelled tickets booked). Ordered most-recent first; returns [] when the parent has organised none.

Sets how the organiser wants ticket proceeds paid out — one or more of Cash / Digital (replaces the previously saved set). **Only valid for PAID events** — a free event returns 400 FreeEventNoPayout. Uses the `eventId` captured by the Paid event create above. 404 EventNotFound; 400 InvalidRequest (empty list / unknown method).

item.name: 9. Event Ticket Bookings
item.description: Buy tickets for an event. Physical events accept any number of tickets (capacity-gated); online events accept exactly ONE ticket per booking (>1 attendee → 400 OnlineEventSingleTicket). One ticket row per attendee.

request.description: paymentMethod ∈ {CreditCard, Twint, Cash, Free}. One ticket row per attendee. Physical = any number of tickets; online = exactly one. Captures `eventBookingId`. 404 EventNotFound; 400 OnlineEventSingleTicket (>1 attendee on an online event); 409 EventSoldOut (physical capacity).
Booking + one entry per ticket (denormalised on the wire).

Soft-cancels the caller's own booking. Authorised by your Firebase **email claim** matched against the booking's bookerEmail (so you can only cancel a booking you made). Flips Status → Cancelled and frees the seat capacity automatically; returns the cancelled booking + tickets. 403 EmailClaimMissing (token has no email); 404 EventBookingNotFound (unknown id OR not yours); 409 EventBookingAlreadyCancelled.

Lists the caller's event-ticket bookings (slim cards joined with the event). Filters by the JWT email claim — 403 EmailClaimMissing if absent. Only matches bookings whose bookerEmail equals your Firebase email. Each card carries the event `eventType` + `eventLocation` (venue address for physical events; null for online), plus title, category, start date/time, and banner.

Streams the raw image bytes for a private-container blob URL (the response is binary — see Postman's Preview tab). Set `blobUrl` to a URL returned by any upload endpoint. 400 InvalidRequest (bad/foreign URL); 404 BlobNotFound.
